tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome, tubular dysfunction, or acute kidney injury. HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or anemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or systemic disease.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
